behaviorsAudited.behavior: App.tsx no longer hardcodes toolJSX={null}; declares useState<ToolJSXState | null>(null) where ToolJSXState matches REPL.tsx:1036 shape ({jsx: React.ReactNode | null, shouldHidePromptInput: boolean, shouldContinueAnimation?: true, showSpinner?: boolean, isLocalJSXCommand?: boolean, isImmediate?: boolean})
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/bridges/use-tool-jsx.ts:14-21 (ToolJSXState type) and runtime/src/tui/bridges/use-tool-jsx.ts:86 (useState<ToolJSXState | null>(null)); consumed by runtime/src/tui/components/App.tsx:167 (const [toolJSX, setToolJSX] = useToolJSX())
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.7 'App.tsx no longer hardcodes toolJSX={null} on <Messages>; the prop is wired to a state variable'

behaviorsAudited.behavior: App.tsx ports the upstream localJSXCommandRef + setToolJSX wrapper from REPL.tsx:1055-1104 so local-JSX commands persist across normal tool updates and only clear on explicit clearLocalJSX:true
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/bridges/use-tool-jsx.ts:48-74 (applyToolJSXUpdate pure function with all 5 branches) and runtime/src/tui/bridges/use-tool-jsx.ts:82-101 (useToolJSX hook with useRef<ToolJSXState | null>(null) at line 87 and setToolJSX wrapper at lines 89-98)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx tests E2.1 (local-set), E2.2 (local-active+skip), E2.3 (local-active+clearLocalJSX), E2.4 (no-local+clearLocalJSX), E2.5 (no-local+normal), E2.6 (null,null), E2.6b (null,localRef)

behaviorsAudited.behavior: The wrapped setToolJSX is exposed to tools through the existing getToolUseContext callback so AgenC tools that render their own UI surfaces can call it
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/components/App.tsx:229-240 (getToolUseContext callback returns object containing setToolJSX at line 237; closure dependency at line 239 includes setToolJSX)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:B2.3 'setToolJSX is exposed via getToolUseContext so AgenC tools can call it'

behaviorsAudited.behavior: When toolJSX !== null, App.tsx renders <Box flexDirection="column" width="100%">{toolJSX.jsx}</Box> as a sibling block, mirroring REPL.tsx:4469-4470
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/components/App.tsx:274-278 (`{toolJSX !== null ? (<Box flexDirection="column" width="100%">{toolJSX.jsx}</Box>) : null}`); rendered as sibling beside <Messages> at App.tsx:258-273
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.8 'App.tsx renders toolJSX.jsx as a sibling Box (matches REPL.tsx:4469-4470)'

behaviorsAudited.behavior: The current toolJSX value is passed verbatim to <Messages toolJSX={...}>, not a normalized copy
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/components/App.tsx:263 (`toolJSX={toolJSX as any}` — `as any` is a TypeScript cast only; no runtime transformation, no spread, no defensive copy)
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.7 (regex /toolJSX\s*=\s*\{\s*toolJSX[^}]*\}/ requires the prop to bind directly to the toolJSX state variable)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: applyToolJSXUpdate(prev=null, args={isLocalJSXCommand:true, jsx:'X', shouldHidePromptInput:false}) stores args (minus clearLocalJSX) in the local-ref equivalent and returns the args as the new state value
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.1 'isLocalJSXCommand:true persists to local-ref AND returns args (minus clearLocalJSX) as next state' — verifies both nextState and nextLocalRef equal {jsx:'X', shouldHidePromptInput:false, isLocalJSXCommand:true} with clearLocalJSX stripped

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is set and args lacks clearLocalJSX returns the previous state unchanged (REPL.tsx:1095 'keep the local JSX command visible')
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.2 'with active local-ref and no clearLocalJSX, normal updates are skipped' — asserts result has skip:true, which (in the hook) avoids calling setToolJSXInternal so prior state is preserved unchanged

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is set and args.clearLocalJSX===true returns null AND clears the local-ref
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.3 'with active local-ref and clearLocalJSX:true, both state and ref are cleared to null' — asserts nextState===null AND nextLocalRef===null

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is null and args.clearLocalJSX===true returns null
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.4 'with no local-ref and clearLocalJSX:true, state goes to null without touching ref' — asserts nextState===null AND nextLocalRef===undefined (ref untouched)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is null and args is a normal value returns args as-is (no transformation)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.5 'with no local-ref and a normal value, args are returned verbatim as next state (no transformation, no ref write)' — uses toBe(incoming) for identity (not just structural equality), nextLocalRef===undefined

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: applyToolJSXUpdate(prev, null) returns null when local-ref is null (full clear path)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.6 'applyToolJSXUpdate(null, null) returns null state (full clear, no local-ref to consider)' — additionally E2.6b covers the localRef-set complement (skip)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: App.tsx source no longer contains the literal hardcoded 'toolJSX={null}' on the <Messages> element; the prop is wired to the toolJSX state variable
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.7 'App.tsx no longer hardcodes toolJSX={null} on <Messages>; the prop is wired to a state variable' — uses regex /toolJSX\s*=\s*\{\s*null\s*\}/ as the negative match and /toolJSX\s*=\s*\{\s*toolJSX[^}]*\}/ as the positive wiring match

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: App.tsx source contains the sibling Box rendering the toolJSX.jsx node when toolJSX is non-null (matches REPL.tsx:4469-4470 exactly)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: App.tooljsx-state.parity.test.tsx:E2.8 'App.tsx renders toolJSX.jsx as a sibling Box (matches REPL.tsx:4469-4470)' — regex requires `toolJSX !== null` (or `!=`) followed by `<Box flexDirection="column" width="100%">` and `{toolJSX.jsx}`

scopeDrift.description: use-tool-jsx.ts splits the upstream wrapper into a pure applyToolJSXUpdate plus a thin useToolJSX hook. Upstream REPL.tsx:1064-1104 inlines the wrapper inside a single useCallback. The split is justified by the matrix language ('applyToolJSXUpdate pure function' is named in the row's task list) and is needed for direct unit testability of the 5 branches without React rendering. Behavioral surface is identical; this is structural-only refactor and is in scope for the row.

unverifiedAssumptions.assumption: The closure dependency list of getToolUseContext at App.tsx:239 includes setToolJSX. Because useToolJSX's setToolJSX is wrapped in useCallback with [] deps (use-tool-jsx.ts:89), its identity is stable across renders, so this dependency does not cause spurious re-creations of the context. The behavior is correct and matches upstream REPL.tsx (where setToolJSX is also useCallback-stable).

ToolJSXArgs in use-tool-jsx.ts:28-35 omits isImmediate?, matching the upstream args type at REPL.tsx:1064-1071 (which also omits it). isImmediate? is a state-only field present on ToolJSXState (use-tool-jsx.ts:20), exactly as upstream models it on the state type at REPL.tsx:1042. The matrix calls out this asymmetry explicitly ('isImmediate? on state, clearLocalJSX? on args only') and the implementation honors it.

summary: All 5 requiredBehaviors and all 8 edgeCases for tui-host-tooljsx-state are implemented and asserted. The ToolJSXState type at use-tool-jsx.ts:14-21 is byte-equivalent to REPL.tsx:1036-1043 (including the asymmetry: isImmediate? on state only, clearLocalJSX? on args only). applyToolJSXUpdate at use-tool-jsx.ts:48-74 mirrors all 5 upstream branches (local-set, local-active+clearLocalJSX, local-active+skip, no-local+clearLocalJSX, no-local+normal) with semantics-preserving control flow. App.tsx:167 wires the hook, App.tsx:237 exposes setToolJSX through getToolUseContext, App.tsx:263 passes toolJSX verbatim to <Messages>, and App.tsx:274-278 renders the sibling <Box flexDirection="column" width="100%">{toolJSX.jsx}</Box> exactly mirroring REPL.tsx:4469-4470. The parity test file passes 10/10 tests on a live vitest run. The only structural deviation from upstream is the pure/hook split, which the matrix explicitly anticipates and which preserves behavior.
